title: briza maxima f. rubra
intro: Briza maxima f. rubra, commonly known as the Red Quaking Grass or Red Wood Millet, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe, known for its striking red or purple foliage and delicate, drooping flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems, skin irritations, and digestive issues. Briza maxima f. rubra can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which have been used to promote relaxation, reduce stress and anxiety, and support overall well-being.
